text:
Courts had already entered default judgments establishing Alex Jones’s liability before the juries awarded damages.

In the Sandy Hook defamation cases:

- Judges in both Connecticut and Texas found Jones liable by default because of repeated failures to comply with discovery and other court orders.
- That meant the jury trials were not primarily about whether he was legally responsible.
- Instead, the jury phase was mainly to determine how much in damages he had to pay, and in some proceedings whether punitive damages or related amounts should be imposed.

So liability was not still open at the jury stage; it had already been decided by the courts through default judgments.
